web.name: SQL Injection
web.description: Exploit SQL injection vulnerabilities to extract data or bypass authentication
steps: Identify injection points (GET/POST parameters, headers, cookies)
Test for error-based injection with single quote
Determine database type from error messages
Use UNION-based or blind injection to extract data
Dump users/credentials table
Read flag file via LOAD_FILE or INTO OUTFILE

web.name: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI)
web.description: Exploit template engine injection to achieve RCE
steps: Identify template injection points with {{7*7}} or ${7*7}
Determine template engine from response (49 vs 7777777)
Identify engine: Jinja2, Twig, Freemarker, Velocity, Mako
Use engine-specific payload to read /etc/passwd
Escalate to RCE via subprocess or os.popen
Read flag file

web.name: Local File Inclusion (LFI)
web.description: Read arbitrary files via path traversal or PHP wrappers
steps: Identify file inclusion parameters (?page=, ?file=, ?include=)
Test path traversal with ../../../../etc/passwd
Try PHP wrappers: php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=
Read source code of application files
Look for credentials, config files, or flag
Escalate to RCE via log poisoning if possible

web.name: Command Injection
web.description: Execute arbitrary OS commands via injection
steps: Identify parameters that may be passed to OS commands
Test with ; id or | id
Confirm execution via time-based blind (sleep 5)
Read /etc/passwd or flag file
Establish reverse shell if needed

web.name: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
web.description: Inject malicious scripts to steal cookies or perform actions
steps: Identify reflection points in the application
Test with <script>alert(1)</script>
Determine context (HTML, attribute, JavaScript)
Craft context-appropriate payload
Steal admin cookie or perform CSRF
Access admin panel to find flag

web.name: XXE Injection
web.description: Exploit XML external entity processing to read files
steps: Identify XML input points (file upload, API endpoints)
Test with basic XXE payload to read /etc/passwd
Try blind XXE with out-of-band data exfiltration
Read application source code or config files
Find flag in common locations

web.name: SSRF
web.description: Make server perform requests to internal resources
steps: Identify URL parameters that fetch remote resources
Test with http://127.0.0.1/ to confirm SSRF
Scan internal network for services
Access cloud metadata endpoint (169.254.169.254)
Access internal admin panels or APIs
Extract credentials or flag

web.name: Authentication Bypass
steps: Test default credentials (admin/admin, admin/password)
Try SQL injection in login form
Check for JWT vulnerabilities (alg:none, weak secret)
Test for insecure direct object references
Check for cookie manipulation
Access admin area to find flag

pwn.name: Buffer Overflow (Stack)
pwn.description: Overflow stack buffer to control return address
steps: Run checksec to identify protections (NX, PIE, canary, RELRO)
Find vulnerable function with gets/strcpy/scanf
Determine offset to return address with cyclic pattern
Check if NX is disabled (ret2shellcode) or enabled (ret2libc/ROP)
Build ROP chain or shellcode
Leak libc base if ASLR enabled
Call system('/bin/sh') or execve
Read flag from shell

pwn.name: Format String
steps: Identify printf(user_input) pattern
Leak stack values with %p.%p.%p
Find offset to control data with %n$p
Leak canary and return address
Overwrite GOT entry or return address with %n
Redirect execution to win function or shellcode

pwn.name: Heap Exploitation
steps: Identify heap operations (malloc/free/realloc)
Find use-after-free, double-free, or heap overflow
Determine libc version for tcache/fastbin attacks
Leak heap and libc addresses
Overwrite __malloc_hook or __free_hook
Get shell and read flag

pwn.name: ret2libc
pwn.description: Return to libc functions to bypass NX
steps: Find buffer overflow vulnerability
Determine offset to return address
Find ROP gadgets (pop rdi; ret)
Leak libc address via puts/printf
Calculate libc base and system() address
Call system('/bin/sh')

crypto.name: RSA Small Exponent (e=3)
crypto.description: Attack RSA with small public exponent
steps: Extract n, e, c from challenge
Check if e is small (3, 5, 17, 65537)
If e=3 and m^3 < n, compute cube root of c
Try Hastad's broadcast attack if multiple ciphertexts
Use RsaCtfTool for automated attacks

crypto.name: RSA Common Modulus
crypto.description: Attack RSA when same message encrypted with same n but different e
steps: Identify two ciphertexts with same n but different e
Verify gcd(e1, e2) == 1
Use extended Euclidean algorithm to find a, b where a*e1 + b*e2 = 1
Compute m = c1^a * c2^b mod n

crypto.name: Classical Cipher Analysis
crypto.description: Break classical ciphers using frequency analysis
steps: Identify cipher type (Caesar, Vigenere, Substitution, Rail Fence)
Perform frequency analysis on ciphertext
Try all 26 Caesar shifts
Use Kasiski test for Vigenere key length
Apply frequency analysis per key position
Decode and find flag

crypto.name: XOR Cipher
crypto.description: Break XOR encryption with known/guessed key
steps: Identify XOR-encrypted data (often hex or base64 encoded)
Try single-byte XOR with all 256 values
Use known plaintext (flag format) to recover key
Try repeating key XOR with Kasiski/IC analysis
Recover full key and decrypt

crypto.name: Hash Cracking
crypto.description: Crack hash values to recover plaintext
steps: Identify hash type (MD5, SHA1, SHA256, bcrypt)
Try online hash databases (crackstation, hashes.org)
Use hashcat or john with wordlists
Try common CTF passwords and flag formats
Use rainbow tables for unsalted hashes

misc.name: Steganography
misc.description: Extract hidden data from image/audio files
steps: Check file metadata with exiftool
Run strings on the file
Try steghide with empty password
Use zsteg for PNG/BMP files
Check LSB steganography manually
Try stegsolve for visual analysis
Check for appended data after EOF

misc.name: File Forensics
misc.description: Analyze files for hidden content or embedded files
steps: Run file command to identify true file type
Use binwalk to extract embedded files
Check file headers and magic bytes
Look for zip/rar archives appended to files
Analyze file structure with hex editor
Extract and analyze embedded content

misc.name: Network Traffic Analysis
misc.description: Analyze PCAP files for hidden data or credentials
steps: Open PCAP in Wireshark
Filter by protocol (HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS)
Follow TCP/HTTP streams
Export HTTP objects
Look for credentials in cleartext protocols
Check DNS queries for data exfiltration
Reconstruct transferred files

misc.name: ZIP/Archive Analysis
misc.description: Crack or bypass archive password protection
steps: Check for pseudo-encryption (zip -e flag without real encryption)
Try common passwords (password, 123456, flag, ctf)
Use john or hashcat with zip2john
Check for plaintext attack if known file inside
Try zipgrep for content search
Extract and analyze contents

misc.name: Encoding/Decoding
misc.description: Decode various encoding schemes to find flag
steps: Identify encoding type (Base64, Hex, Binary, Morse, etc.)
Try CyberChef magic detection
Decode layer by layer (multiple encodings)
Try common CTF encodings: Base32, Base58, Base85
Check for custom encoding schemes
Decode to find flag

reverse.name: Static Analysis
steps: Run file and checksec on binary
Extract strings with strings command
Disassemble with objdump or radare2
Decompile with Ghidra or IDA
Identify main logic and flag checking function
Understand algorithm and reverse it
Compute correct input/flag

reverse.name: Dynamic Analysis
steps: Run binary with strace/ltrace to trace syscalls/library calls
Use GDB to set breakpoints at key functions
Trace execution flow with pwndbg/peda
Identify flag comparison location
Patch binary or extract flag from memory
Use angr for symbolic execution if needed

reverse.name: Constraint Solving (Z3/Angr)
reverse.description: Use symbolic execution or SMT solver to find valid inputs
steps: Identify input validation logic
Model constraints in Z3 or use angr symbolic execution
Define input variables and constraints
Solve for satisfying assignment
Verify solution against binary

reverse.name: Anti-Debug Bypass
steps: Identify anti-debug checks (ptrace, IsDebuggerPresent, timing)
Patch binary to skip checks
Use LD_PRELOAD to hook ptrace
Set hardware breakpoints to avoid software detection
Use ScyllaHide plugin for transparent debugging

reverse.name: Python Bytecode Reverse
reverse.description: Reverse engineer Python bytecode or compiled scripts
steps: Identify .pyc files or Python-compiled executables
Use uncompyle6 or decompile3 to decompile .pyc
For PyInstaller: use pyinstxtractor to extract
Analyze decompiled Python source
Understand flag generation logic
Reverse or brute-force the flag
